description: Ensure SSR output and first client render use the same data and browser-only APIs are gated.
body: Use when changing Nuxt pages, layouts, plugins, composables, or data fetching. Do not access window/document/localStorage during server render. Use Nuxt data/lifecycle conventions and client-only gates where appropriate. Verify build/dev logs, hydration console, and a browser reload. Risk boundary includes SEO-visible markup and user/session data leaks. Use when Vue Frontend Engineering work touches visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
